title: Biasolettia divaricata
intro: Biasolettia divaricata, also known as Wild Fennel or Yellow Wild Fennel, is a perennial herbaceous plant native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its yellow flowers and feathery foliage. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including the relief of digestive issues, reduction of inflammation, and improvement of respiratory conditions. The bioactive constituents of Biasolettia divaricata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal effects. Herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat gastrointestinal issues and promote overall well-being.

The classification of Biasolettia divaricata includes being a member of the order Gentianales.
